---
title: "Corvus Star Phase 5 Implementation Backlog"
date: 2026-03-09
status: completed
---

# Corvus Star Phase 5 Implementation Backlog

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This backlog operationalizes Phase 5 from `docs/architecture/WOVEN_SKILL_CONSOLIDATION_BLUEPRINT.qmd`.
> Phase 5 stabilizes PennyOne against the canonical schema.
> PennyOne may continue to emit compatibility artifacts, but visualization and search can no longer treat temporary graph files as primary authority.

## Phase 5 Mission

Phase 5 exists to make PennyOne a downstream consumer of Hall state rather than a mixed system that sometimes reads Hall and sometimes trusts temporary graph artifacts.

The required outcomes are:

1. PennyOne matrix views are materialized from Hall-backed projections.
2. Runtime and visualization surfaces stop treating `matrix-graph.json` as live authority.
3. Temporary graph files are compatibility exports only.
4. PennyOne consumers use one stable projection contract instead of ad hoc graph assumptions.
5. Matrix visualization remains downstream from scans, telemetry, and Hall writes.

## Phase 5 Exit Criteria

- A stable PennyOne projection contract exists for Hall-backed matrix views.
- The vis proxy can serve matrix state from Hall projections without reading raw graph files as authority.
- Scan, telemetry, and visualization invalidation no longer depend on `matrix-graph.json` as the live source of truth.
- Temporary graph artifacts are clearly compatibility-only exports.
- PennyOne consumers rely on one canonical projection surface for matrix state.

## Execution Status

Current Phase 5 state:

- Phase 1 completed on 2026-03-09
- Phase 2 completed on 2026-03-09
- Phase 3 completed on 2026-03-09
- Phase 4 completed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P5-00` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P5-01` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P5-02` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P5-03` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P5-04` closed on 2026-03-09
- PennyOne now serves visualization state, Warden evaluation, and search from Hall-backed projections
- `matrix-graph.json` remains only as an explicit compatibility export
- Phase 5 completed on 2026-03-09

## Ticket Index

| ID | Status | Priority | Ticket | Depends On | Primary Surfaces |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `CS-P5-00` | Closed | P0 | PennyOne Projection Contract Freeze | Phase 4 complete | `pennyone/types.ts`, `intel/compiler.ts`, `vis/proxy.ts`, projection tests |
| `CS-P5-01` | Closed | P0 | Remove Graph File Authority From Visualization Transport | `CS-P5-00` | `vis/proxy.ts`, live broadcast surfaces, PennyOne vis tests |
| `CS-P5-02` | Closed | P1 | Make Scan And Warden Flows Projection-First | `CS-P5-00` | `pennyone/index.ts`, `intel/warden.ts`, scan tests |
| `CS-P5-03` | Closed | P1 | Harden Consumers Against Temporary Graph Assumptions | `CS-P5-00` | `vis/logic`, `live/search.ts`, consumer tests |
| `CS-P5-04` | Closed | P1 | Quarantine Legacy Matrix Artifact Paths | `CS-P5-01`, `CS-P5-02`, `CS-P5-03` | compatibility exports, legacy tests, docs |

## Critical Path

The critical path is:

`CS-P5-00 -> CS-P5-01 -> CS-P5-02 -> CS-P5-03 -> CS-P5-04`

## Ticket Details

## `CS-P5-00` PennyOne Projection Contract Freeze

### Objective

Freeze one canonical PennyOne matrix projection contract before removing the remaining graph-file authority leaks.

### Files

- `src/tools/pennyone/types.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/compiler.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/vis/proxy.ts`
- `tests/unit/test_gungnir_schema.test.ts`
- `tests/unit/test_pennyone_projection_boundary.test.ts`

### Change Set

- Define a canonical Hall-backed PennyOne projection envelope.
- Add one loader that materializes the current matrix view from Hall.
- Route the vis proxy matrix API through that loader instead of raw file reads.
- Keep `matrix-graph.json` only as a compatibility export.

### Acceptance Criteria

- PennyOne has one canonical loader for Hall-backed matrix projection state.
- The vis proxy can serve matrix state without directly reading `matrix-graph.json`.
- Projection payloads clearly indicate that Hall is the authority.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_gungnir_schema.test.ts tests/unit/test_pennyone_projection_boundary.test.ts
npx tsc --noEmit
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- `compiler.ts` now exposes a Hall-backed matrix projection reader in addition to the compatibility writer.
- PennyOne projection payloads now declare their authority and artifact role explicitly.
- The vis proxy now serves `/api/matrix` from Hall-backed projection materialization rather than direct JSON artifact reads.

## `CS-P5-01` Remove Graph File Authority From Visualization Transport

### Objective

Move visualization invalidation and refresh behavior off the compatibility graph file.

### Files

- `src/tools/pennyone/vis/proxy.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/live/recorder.ts`
- PennyOne vis transport tests

### Change Set

- Stop watching `matrix-graph.json` as the primary refresh signal.
- Broadcast matrix refresh from Hall/DB mutations or explicit projection rebuild events.
- Ensure visualization transport remains downstream from canonical writes.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Visualization refresh no longer depends on graph-file mutation as the primary signal.
- PennyOne vis updates are downstream from canonical Hall-backed state changes.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsx --test tests/empire_tests/test_pennyone_phase4_empire.ts tests/unit/test_pennyone_projection_boundary.test.ts
npx tsc --noEmit
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- PennyOne live transport now emits `MATRIX_UPDATED` from canonical write activity instead of graph-file rebuild semantics.
- Watcher tests now inject their scan runner and verify refresh signaling without depending on raw graph rebuild behavior.
- The vis proxy stays downstream from Hall and `.stats` mutations rather than treating `matrix-graph.json` as a primary signal.

## `CS-P5-02` Make Scan And Warden Flows Projection-First

### Objective

Ensure scan and threat-assessment flows operate on canonical projection state rather than ad hoc runtime graph payloads.

### Files

- `src/tools/pennyone/index.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/warden.ts`
- Scan and Warden tests

### Change Set

- Use Hall-backed projection materialization as the authoritative post-scan matrix surface.
- Demote runtime graph payloads to transient internal analysis only where still needed.
- Remove scan metadata that implies compatibility graph artifacts are authoritative.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Post-scan PennyOne authority is Hall-backed projection state.
- Warden and related flows no longer require a temporary graph artifact as their primary input.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_pennyone_projection_gate.test.ts tests/unit/test_pennyone_projection_boundary.test.ts tests/unit/test_gungnir_schema.test.ts
npx tsc --noEmit
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- `runScan()` now writes Hall, materializes the compatibility export, and then invokes Warden against the Hall-backed projection.
- `Warden` now exposes an explicit projection-evaluation entrypoint so threat assessment reads canonical state instead of transient scan payloads.
- Scan metadata now records Hall projection authority and demotes `matrix-graph.json` to compatibility export status.

## `CS-P5-03` Harden Consumers Against Temporary Graph Assumptions

### Objective

Make PennyOne consumers depend only on the stable projection contract, not scanner-era graph quirks.

### Files

- `src/tools/pennyone/vis/logic/`
- `src/tools/pennyone/live/search.ts`
- Consumer tests

### Change Set

- Remove implicit assumptions about temporary graph-only fields.
- Normalize consumer expectations around the canonical projection envelope.
- Preserve visualization behavior when compatibility fields are absent or reduced.

### Acceptance Criteria

- PennyOne consumers tolerate the canonical projection contract without graph-era assumptions.
- Search and visualization continue to operate from Hall-derived projection state.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_pennyone_consumer_contract.test.ts tests/empire_tests/test_pennyone_search_empire.ts
npx tsc --noEmit
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- PennyOne vis normalization now accepts the canonical projection envelope and no longer relies on graph-era field placement for interaction protocol data.
- Search tests now exercise Hall-backed projection state directly instead of skipping when `matrix-graph.json` is absent.
- Consumer hardening tests now cover reduced compatibility payloads so the UI continues to function when graph-era extras disappear.

## `CS-P5-04` Quarantine Legacy Matrix Artifact Paths

### Objective

Collapse or quarantine the remaining code paths that still treat compatibility graph exports as live operating surfaces.

### Files

- Remaining compatibility graph readers and watchers
- Legacy PennyOne tests
- Phase 5 closure docs

### Change Set

- Quarantine or excise graph-only compatibility surfaces that remain outside the canonical projection path.
- Leave only explicit export paths for temporary graph artifacts.
- Document the final downstream-only PennyOne authority model.

### Acceptance Criteria

- No live PennyOne authority path depends on `matrix-graph.json`.
- Compatibility graph exports remain optional and clearly non-authoritative.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsx --test tests/empire_tests/test_pennyone_phase4_empire.ts tests/unit/test_pennyone_projection_boundary.test.ts tests/unit/test_pennyone_projection_gate.test.ts tests/unit/test_pennyone_consumer_contract.test.ts tests/empire_tests/test_pennyone_search_empire.ts
npx tsc --noEmit
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- The old graph-integrity test was quarantined because it treated `matrix-graph.json` as a live operating surface.
- Checked-in vis graph artifacts were excised from the source tree.
- Remaining `matrix-graph.json` references are confined to the explicit compatibility export path and compatibility-boundary tests.
